content.text:
REVIEW-FINDING: 5.1.1(i) WARN — Privacy policy states "We do not collect, process, or store your location data" but RunSessionManager.swift:1-16 imports CoreLocation, calls requestWhenInUseAuthorization(), startUpdatingLocation(), and stores route history, while description.txt explicitly advertises "maps every run with GPS".

Pierre: Apple's reviewers directly cross-check the fetched privacy policy against actual code behavior and store copy, and a flat denial of location collection alongside a CoreLocation-based GPS tracking feature is a textbook contradiction that can trigger rejection or app removal. This is worse than a generic omission — it's an explicit false statement. Fix the privacy policy to accurately disclose location collection/usage (and retention/sync behavior), and make sure App Privacy nutrition labels and PrivacyInfo.xcprivacy reflect the same.
